_comment: Group-based conflict matrix. A rule fires when at least one present ingredient from each of its two groups is found. Group members are canonical INCI keys from ingredient_master.json; members not yet in the ledger stay dormant until added.

rules.reason: Extreme risk of over-exfoliation, barrier damage, and redness.
Can severely dry out the skin and compromise the moisture barrier.
Benzoyl peroxide oxidizes and degrades traditional retinol, rendering both less effective.
Best used at different times of day due to differing optimal pH and combined irritation risk.
Both are acidic; combining can cause irritation and peeling, though some resilient skin types tolerate it.
Both are acidic; combining can cause irritation and peeling, though some resilient skin types tolerate it.
Benzoyl peroxide oxidizes vitamin C, making it largely ineffective.
Copper can oxidize L-ascorbic acid, reducing its antioxidant benefit.
Contested; older studies suggested flushing, but modern formulations are generally stable.
The low pH of acids can hydrolyze certain peptide bonds, reducing their effectiveness.
The low pH of acids can hydrolyze certain peptide bonds, reducing their effectiveness.
Greatly increases skin sensitivity; combining directly can cause contact dermatitis.
Greatly increases skin sensitivity; combining directly can cause contact dermatitis.
Exfoliants compromise the barrier, letting fragrance allergens penetrate deeper and irritate.
Exfoliants compromise the barrier, letting fragrance allergens penetrate deeper and irritate.
Retinoids compromise the barrier slightly, letting fragrance allergens penetrate deeper and irritate.
